job_title: Software Engineer III, Infrastructure, Google Cloud Performance
job_nlp_title: Software Engineer III, Infrastructure, Google Cloud Performance
taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Software Engineering, Infrastructure Engineer
core_skills: C++, Python, Go, Large-scale infrastructure development, Distributed systems
skill_match_scores: C++:0.0, Python:1.0, Go:1.0, Large-scale infrastructure development:0.0, Distributed systems:0.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience, 2 years of experience programming in C++, Python or Go, 2 years of experience with developing large-scale infrastructure, distributed systems or networks, or experience with compute technologies, storage or hardware architecture
preferred_quals: Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science or related technical fields, 2 years of experience with performance, large scale systems data analysis, visualization tools, or debugging, 2 years of experience with data structures or algorithms in either an academic or industry setting, Experience developing accessible technologies, Proficiency in code and system health, diagnosis and resolution, and software test engineering
core_responsibilities: Write product or system development code, Participate in, or lead design reviews with peers and stakeholders to decide amongst available technologies, Review code developed by other developers and provide feedback to ensure best practices (e.g., style guidelines, checking code in, accuracy, testability, and efficiency), Contribute to existing documentation or educational content and adapt content based on product/program updates and user feedback, Triage product or system issues and debug/track/resolve by analyzing the sources of issues and the impact on hardware, network, or service operations and quality
job_summary: Google is a leading technology company known for its innovative solutions and products. They are seeking a Software Engineer III to develop next-generation technologies and maintain large-scale infrastructure, ensuring optimal performance and reliability for Google Cloud services.
